name: spi_master
description: SPI master that shifts words over MOSI/MISO with configurable clock polarity, phase, divider, and chip select.

source_refs.license: OpenCores project license; verify file header before reuse
OpenCores project license; verify file header before reuse
OpenCores project license; verify file header before reuse

states.name: IDLE
states.description: SCLK at idle polarity, chip select inactive.

states.name: ASSERT_CS
states.description: Select target before first clock edge.

states.name: TRANSFER
states.description: Toggle SCLK, shift MOSI, sample MISO according to CPOL/CPHA.

states.name: DEASSERT_CS
states.description: Finish word and release chip select.

constraints: SCLK idle value must match CPOL.
Launch and sample edges must match CPHA for the selected mode.
Chip select must cover the complete transfer word unless continuous/burst mode is explicitly implemented.
Avoid using internally generated SCLK as a fabric clock when a clock-enable strobe is sufficient.
patterns: Use a divider counter to derive SCLK enable strobes rather than a generated clock when possible.
Separate launch and sample edges to support CPHA modes.
Shift TX and RX registers in opposite directions according to bit order.
Keep chip select asserted across the whole word and optionally across bursts.
implementation_notes: Use a divider counter to generate SCLK edge enables from clk.
Keep TX and RX shift registers plus a bit counter sized for DATA_WIDTH.
Separate edge phase logic from shift register update logic to keep CPOL/CPHA clear.
Pulse done after the final sample edge and deassert busy when chip select can be released.
test_strategy: Loop MOSI back to MISO and check rx_data equals tx_data.
Exercise CPOL/CPHA combinations on edge timing.
Check cs_n assertion window and done pulse.
verification_goals: Loopback MOSI to MISO returns the transmitted word.
All four CPOL/CPHA modes produce expected launch/sample edge ordering.
cs_n is asserted before the first active edge and released after the final edge.
done pulses once per completed word.
